faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants vs regular ants in my East Meadow home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than regular ants (up to 5/8 inch long), are typically black or dark brown, and leave behind piles of wood shavings called frass. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests. If you notice large ants, especially near wooden structures, or hear rustling sounds in your walls, you likely have carpenter ants.
